violations.text: Several food items were observed not marked with the date of preparation. Ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods prepared in the establishment and held for more than 24 hours must be marked with the date of preparation. New Violation. Correct By: 04/14/2025
Utensils were observed stored in stagnant water between uses. Utensils were removed and properly stored at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/14/2025

Pans used for cooking were observed stored with the food contact surface facing the prep counter directly behind them. Equipment is to be stored in a way that prevents the contamination of surfaces at times of prep. Equipment was adjusted for proper storage at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/14/2025

The facility is lacking a mop sink. At least one mop sink or one curbed cleaning facility equipped with a floor drain must be provided and conveniently located for the cleaning of mops or similar wet floor cleaning tools and for the disposal of mop water and similar liquid waste. New Violation. Correct By: 04/14/2025
Several floor tiles in the kitchen area were observed to be cracked and chipped and in poor repair. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 10/14/2025

2 pans of Coney Island Sauce, which was previously cooked and cooled, was only reheated to 77 and 101 degrees Fahrenheit for hot holding in the steam unit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food that is cooked, cooled, and reheated for hot holding must be reheated to 165 degrees Fahrenheit or above for 15 seconds. Proper strategies for proper reheating were discussed at the time of inspection. Coney Island Sauce was properly reheated to 185 at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 09/03/2024

Utensils were observed stored in stagnant water between uses. Strategies for proper storage of in-use utensils were discussed at the time of inspection. Utensils placed in hot water in the hot hold unit at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 09/03/2024

A saucepan observed stored on the clean storage rack was observed to have a flaking, noncleanable surface and in poor repair. PIC elected to disposed of the pan at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 09/03/2024
The facility is lacking thermo-labels or similar internal temperature indicating device for the dish machine in the kitchen area. New Violation. Correct By: 09/17/2024
The drain to the back kitchen hand sink was observed to be leaking when in use. Plumbing system is to be adjusted and maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 09/17/2024

The facility is lacking a mop sink. At least one mop sink or one curbed cleaning facility equipped with a floor drain must be provided and conveniently located for the cleaning of mops or similar wet floor cleaning tools and for the disposal of mop water and similar liquid waste. New Violation. Correct By: 10/01/2024

Several floor tiles in the kitchen area were observed to be cracked and chipped and in poor repair. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. Per the PIC, the owner has acquired tile for replacements. New Violation. Correct By: 12/31/2024
An allergy statement is lacking on the menus. Until new menus are printed establishment to put allergy statement at the counter where customers walk in. New Violation. Correct By: 07/10/2023

No person certified in food safety was present as needed at the time of inspection. Establishment to have a food safety manager at all times food preparation is occurring. New Violation. Correct By: 07/24/2023

All foods were observed not marked with the date of preparation. Ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods prepared in the establishment and held for more than 24 hours must be marked with the date of preparation. New Violation. Correct By: 07/10/2023

Thermometers are lacking in the refrigeration units. Cold holding equipment used for time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods must have at least one thermometer that is located to allow easy viewing of the temperature display. New Violation. Correct By: 07/10/2023
The non-food contact surfaces of the ice machine have an accumulation of biofilm. Per PIC, ice machine is due for its monthly cleaning. New Violation. Correct By: 07/10/2023
